title: Agent Engineering: Making AI Agents Production-Ready
author: Deep concept
text:
Member-only story
Agent Engineering: Making AI Agents Production-Ready
Discover how agent engineering is reshaping the way we build, ship, and scale reliable LLM-based agents in real-world production systems.
Agent Engineering: The Discipline Redefining Modern Software
If you’ve ever shipped an AI agent to production, you know this truth too well: the jump from “it works in dev” to “it behaves in prod” is not a gap — it’s a chasm.
In traditional software development, we mostly know the inputs. We define expected outputs. We test against edge cases. But agents — powered by large language models — don’t play by these rules. Users can say literally anything. Outputs aren’t always predictable. And the space of possible behaviors is infinite. That’s the power of LLMs — and also the risk.
Over the past few years, teams across industries have confronted this head-on. The ones who’ve succeeded — Clay, Vanta, LinkedIn, Cloudflare — aren’t just writing prompts or deploying chatbots. They’re pioneering a new craft: Agent Engineering.
Let’s explore what this emerging discipline actually means — and why it might become a defining pillar of AI development.
